CASPULE Bond Network Report

pbg-caspule wraps CASPULE — a modified LAMMPS that adds fix bond/create/random on top of LAMMPS's built-in fix bond/create and fix bond/break — as a process-bigraph Process. The wrapper queries the live bond list at every update step and reports it through dedicated PBG ports: the bond list, per-type counts, formed/broken event counts, bond energy, and connected-component cluster sizes. The three experiments below show the wrapper driving polymer self-assembly, sticker-spacer condensation, and steady-state associative bond turnover.

Self-Assembly PolymerizationSticker–Spacer CondensateAssociative Network Turnover
1

Self-Assembly Polymerization

Free monomers → linear chains via fix bond/create

A box of 500 randomly-placed monomers polymerises into linear chains and rings. Each atom is capped at two bonds (`iparam 2 1 jparam 2 1`), so branching is forbidden — the network grows strictly as 1D filaments. The `cluster_sizes` port directly reports the chain-length distribution as it evolves from a monomer soup to a fibrous gel.

Atoms500
Bonds (start → end)0 → 482
Total bonds482
Formed (total)482
Broken (total)0
Final clusters26largest = 81
Snapshots31
Runtime0.23s

LAMMPS Input Script

Click to expand the full script the wrapper fed to LAMMPS 21 lines · 1 create_atoms · 0 create_bonds · 3 fix 
units lj
atom_style bond
boundary p p p
neighbor 0.5 bin
neigh_modify every 1 delay 0 check yes
region box block 0 9.0 0 9.0 0 9.0
create_box 1 box bond/types 1 extra/bond/per/atom 2 extra/special/per/atom 30
mass 1 1.0
create_atoms 1 random 500 12345 box overlap 0.85 maxtry 100
pair_style soft 1.6
pair_coeff * * 25.0
bond_style harmonic
bond_coeff 1 5.0 1.2
minimize 1e-4 1e-4 300 300
fix nve all nve/limit 0.05
fix lan all langevin 0.6 0.6 1.0 12345
timestep 0.005
# Form bonds at <1.4σ; max 2 bonds per atom -> linear chains/rings only
fix mkbnd all bond/create 4 1 1 1.4 1 iparam 2 1 jparam 2 1
thermo 5

3D Bond Network Viewer

Sticker (type 1)
Spacer (type 2)
Backbone
Crosslink
Drag to rotate · Scroll to zoom
t = 0

Bond Network & Cluster Dynamics

Bigraph Architecture

Composite Document

2

Sticker–Spacer Condensate

Pre-built chains crosslink into a percolating gel

12 heteropolymer chains, each 12 beads long with the repeating pattern sticker (type 1) – spacer – spacer, are arranged on a grid. Backbone bonds (type 1) hold each chain together; sticker–sticker crosslinks (type 2) form dynamically across chains. Within ~2 simulation-time units the 12 separate clusters merge into a single percolating condensate — exactly the use case CASPULE’s `bond/create/random` was designed for.

Atoms144
Bonds (start → end)132 → 177
Crosslinks (type 2)45
Formed (total)45
Broken (total)0
Final clusters1largest = 144
Snapshots31
Runtime0.02s

LAMMPS Input Script

Click to expand the full script the wrapper fed to LAMMPS 297 lines · 144 create_atoms · 132 create_bonds · 3 fix 
units lj
atom_style bond
boundary p p p
neighbor 0.5 bin
neigh_modify every 1 delay 0 check yes
region box block 0 16.40 0 9.20 0 7.40
create_box 2 box bond/types 2 extra/bond/per/atom 8 extra/special/per/atom 100
mass * 1.0
create_atoms 1 single 1.000 0.900 0.900
create_atoms 2 single 2.200 0.900 0.900

group stickers type 1
pair_style soft 1.5
pair_coeff * * 15.0
bond_style harmonic
bond_coeff 1 30.0 1.2
bond_coeff 2 8.0 1.5
minimize 1e-4 1e-4 200 200
fix nve all nve/limit 0.05
fix lan all langevin 0.8 0.8 1.0 54321
timestep 0.005
# Stickers crosslink (bond type 2) at <1.5σ; up to 3 bonds per sticker
fix mkbnd stickers bond/create 4 1 1 1.5 2 iparam 3 1 jparam 3 1
thermo 5

3D Bond Network Viewer

Sticker (type 1)
Spacer (type 2)
Backbone
Crosslink
Drag to rotate · Scroll to zoom
t = 0

Bond Network & Cluster Dynamics

Bigraph Architecture

Composite Document

3

Associative Network Turnover

fix bond/create + fix bond/break in steady state

48 short bifunctional chains (sticker–spacer³–sticker), packed densely on a grid. Crosslink bonds (type 2) form stochastically between stickers within 1.5σ and break stochastically beyond 2.1σ. After the initial gelation transient the crosslink count plateaus while individual bonds keep turning over — the wrapper reports both the steady-state count and the per-step formation/breaking event counts.

Atoms240
Bonds (start → end)192 → 311
Crosslinks (type 2)119
Formed (total)121
Broken (total)2
Final clusters2largest = 160
Snapshots31
Runtime0.04s

LAMMPS Input Script

Click to expand the full script the wrapper fed to LAMMPS 454 lines · 240 create_atoms · 192 create_bonds · 4 fix 
units lj
atom_style bond
boundary p p p
neighbor 0.5 bin
neigh_modify every 1 delay 0 check yes
region box block 0 21.20 0 8.40 0 8.40
create_box 2 box bond/types 2 extra/bond/per/atom 8 extra/special/per/atom 200
mass * 1.0
create_atoms 1 single 1.000 0.800 0.800
create_atoms 2 single 2.200 0.800 0.800
create_atoms 2 single 3.400 0.800 0.800

create_bonds single/bond 1 239 240
group stickers type 1
pair_style soft 1.5
pair_coeff * * 12.0
bond_style harmonic
bond_coeff 1 30.0 1.2
bond_coeff 2 8.0 1.4
minimize 1e-4 1e-4 200 200
fix nve all nve/limit 0.05
fix lan all langevin 0.7 0.7 1.0 67890
timestep 0.005
# Crosslinks form at <1.5σ (prob 0.6) and break beyond 2.1σ (prob 0.15)
fix mkbnd stickers bond/create 4 1 1 1.5 2 iparam 4 1 jparam 4 1 prob 0.6 475231
fix brkbnd all bond/break 8 2 2.1 prob 0.15 746793
thermo 10
